title: Wall-Mounted Christmas Tree Display
summary: Check out how to make a DIY flush mount wall Christmas tree!
description: Check out how to make a DIY flush mount wall Christmas tree! This is a perfect option for tight spaces to display ornaments or decor! You could even use this as your Christmas tree for minimalist style!

steps.title: Cuts and pocket holes
steps.body: - Cut down your board into 2" wide, or cut down a strip of plywood. - Cut all the beveled angles on each piece. - Drill all your pocket holes at once. - Drill two pocket holes in each end of all the Side and Top Right pieces. - Drill two pocket holes on one end of the Top Left piece.

steps.title: Create tree layers
steps.body: - Using wood glue and pocket hole screws, connect two of the Side pieces on top of Base 1, flush against the angled edges so the angles align. - Clamp down on these boards because you're drilling at an angle and you don't want the boards to slip! - Repeat these steps for the next two layers using Base 2 and Base 3.

steps.title: Connect layers
steps.body: - Using wood glue and pocket hole screws, attach the Sides connected to Base 1 into the bottom of Base 2. These should be 1 1/4" away from the ends. - Repeat to connect this unit into the bottom of Base 3 the same way.

steps.title: Build Top
steps.body: - Using wood glue and pocket hole screws, attach the Top Left into the top of Base 4 so that the angles align on the left side. - Using wood glue and pocket hole screws, attach the Top Right into Base 4 and the Top Left.

steps.title: Connect Top
steps.body: - Using wood glue and pocket hole screws, attach the Sides connected to Base 3 into the bottom of Base 1. These should be 1 1/4" away from the ends.

steps.title: Fill Pocket Holes
steps.body: - If you’d like, use wood glue and Kreg pocket hole plugs to fill all the pocket holes and conceal them. - Once dry, use a flush cut saw to cut them flush with the sides and then sand smooth.

steps.title: Stain
steps.body: - Apply your favorite color of wood stain and a layer of polyurethane to your Christmas tree.

steps.title: Hang
steps.body: - Attach wall hangers to the back of the Sides in the third section. - Add brackets to the wall and then hang your tree. - Decorate with Christmas lights, ornaments or any holiday decor you’d like!

steps.title: Enjoy!
steps.body: Enjoy your handiwork and tag @CharlestonCrafted on Instagram with a picture!
